rulings.Case Title: JAMES PETERSON JR VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 23, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Pierce Lathing Co.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is off calendar per written request of moving party. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES PETERSON JR VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Lehigh Southwest Cement Companys Motion For Leave To Amend Its Expert Designation Pursuant To 2034.720
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 23, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Lehigh Southwest Cement Company's Motion for an Order Granting Leave to Amend its Expert Designation Pursuant to CCP § 2034.720 is denied. Defendant failed to submit a declaration showing that Shannon H. Gaffney was omitted from its expert designation as a result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. (CCP § 2034.720 (c)(1).) Defendant also failed to file Exhibits A-C to the declaration of Daniel Trowbridge in support of its motion and failed to provide the Court with courtesy copies of Exhibits A-C pursuant to Local Rule 2.7(b). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT FOSTER ET AL VS. CRANE CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Substituting Successor-In-Interest For Deceased Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 23, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Substituting Successor-In-Interest for Deceased Plaintiff is granted. Laura Foster is substituted as successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff, Robert Foster. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court.
